heading: Artist Portal Help
intro: Find step-by-step guides, feature explanations, and troubleshooting for every area of the {labelShortName} Artist Portal. Use search or browse by category to jump directly to the topic you need.
search_placeholder: Search help topics, features, and glossary…
search_no_results: No matching help articles. Try different keywords or browse categories below.
glossary_intro: Quick definitions for terms used throughout the Artist Portal, analytics, and finance workflows.
palette_description: Open the command palette to search all help topics and glossary entries from anywhere in the portal.
palette_tooltip: Press Ctrl+K (or Cmd+K on Mac) to open help search
topic_overview_overview_body: The portal dashboard is your home screen after sign-in. It summarizes the active artist, key performance indicators, and shortcuts to the most important tasks such as completing your profile, reviewing releases, or checking pending label messages. Data on this page reflects the currently selected artist when you manage more than one act. Refresh the page or switch artists if numbers look stale after a recent update.
topic_overview_intelligence_body: The intelligence section surfaces actionable insights drawn from your catalog, analytics, and portal activity. It may highlight incomplete profile fields, releases awaiting action, upcoming events, or finance items that need attention. Each insight links to the relevant portal page so you can resolve it in one click. Insights update as you complete tasks or as label data syncs overnight.
topic_overview_pages_body: From the dashboard you can preview how fans and press see you on {labelShortName}. Links open your public artist page, fan landing page, and EPK share URL in a new tab without leaving the portal. Use previews after profile or fan-page edits to confirm photos, bios, and links render correctly. Preview links respect publish state—draft fan pages may not be visible until approved.
topic_overview_completion_body: The completion meter tracks required and recommended profile fields such as photo, biography, booking contact, and streaming links. A higher completion score improves discoverability on the label site and feeds data into the EPK builder automatically. Click any missing item in the checklist to jump to the correct profile tab. Completion is per artist, not per user account.
topic_overview_usecase_body: Open the dashboard at the start of each session to see what needs attention before diving into detailed tools. Managers with multiple artists use it as a roster health check after switching acts. Before a release campaign or tour, verify completion and intelligence items so press, analytics, and billing stay aligned. Bookmark the dashboard if you frequently return to the same artist context.
topic_overview_troubleshoot_body: If the dashboard shows “no artist linked,” your account is not yet connected to an artist profile—complete onboarding or accept a label invite. Empty KPI cards usually mean analytics or finance features are disabled for your act; see Feature flags help. If insights never update, confirm you are viewing the correct artist in the header switcher. Persistent errors after refresh may indicate a temporary outage; try again later or contact your label contact.
topic_analytics_overview_body: Analytics consolidates streaming, listener, territory, earnings, and engagement data for the active artist in one workspace. Tabs group related metrics so you can move from high-level trends to release-level or territory-level detail without exporting spreadsheets first. Most charts respect the date range and filters set in the toolbar. Data freshness depends on distributor sync and label uploads—some tabs may lag by 24–48 hours.
topic_analytics_toolbar_body: Use the toolbar to narrow all visible tabs by date range, release, territory, or other dimensions available for your catalog. Quick search jumps to a release or track name within large rosters. Export generates CSV files for the current view, useful for accounting, tour planning, or sharing with your team. Reset filters when numbers look unexpectedly empty—an overly narrow range is the most common cause.
topic_analytics_tab_streaming_body: The Streaming tab shows plays, streams, and trends over time across connected platforms. Compare periods to spot growth around releases, playlist adds, or tour dates. Drill into individual tracks or releases when the table supports row expansion. Use this tab first when answering “how is the catalog performing overall?”
topic_analytics_tab_listeners_body: Listeners combines third-party audience signals such as Last.fm and Soundcharts where configured for your artist. You may see listener counts, affinity metrics, or chart positions depending on data availability. This tab helps contextualize streaming spikes with broader fan-base indicators. If a source is missing, the label may not have linked that integration yet.
topic_analytics_tab_territories_body: Territories breaks down performance by country or region so you can prioritize marketing and touring. Maps and tables show share of streams, earnings, or growth by territory for the selected period. Use territory data to plan geo-targeted ads, playlist pitching, and show routing. Zero rows for a country often means no qualifying activity in that window rather than a sync error.
topic_analytics_tab_events_body: The Events tab correlates concerts, festivals, and promo milestones with streaming and engagement movement. Overlay show dates on charts to see whether live activity lifted catalog plays in nearby markets. Promo markers may include playlist campaigns logged in Marketing. This view supports post-tour reporting to venues and sponsors.
topic_analytics_tab_earnings_body: Earnings summarizes revenue attributed to streaming, downloads, and other royalty-bearing activity in the selected period. Figures are informational and may differ slightly from Statements of Sales (SOS) after adjustments, splits, or recoupment. Use earnings for trend analysis; use Statements for official payout amounts. Currency display follows your label configuration.
topic_analytics_tab_releases_body: Releases ranks your catalog items by streams, revenue, or growth for the active filter set. Identify which singles, EPs, or albums drive the majority of activity. Click through to related detail when available to compare tracks within a release. Helpful when deciding which back-catalog titles to promote during a tour.
topic_analytics_tab_revenue_mix_body: Revenue mix shows how income splits across sources such as streaming platforms, downloads, sync, or other channels present in your data. Pie or stacked charts make it easy to see dependency on a single DSP. Use this when discussing strategy with your label or planning diversification. Missing slices indicate channels with no reported revenue in the period.
topic_analytics_tab_epk_press_body: This tab tracks engagement with your electronic press kit and shared press assets where tracking is enabled. View opens, downloads, or link clicks to measure press campaign effectiveness. Spikes often follow interview publication or festival announcement timing. Low activity may prompt updating EPK content or refreshing share links.
topic_analytics_tab_settlement_body: The settlement ledger displays running balances, carry-forwards, and tour-related settlements when finance modules are active. It connects show-level income and expenses to your broader royalty picture. Review before approving tour merch settlements or issuing invoices against SOS periods. Ledger entries should align with Statements once periods are finalized.
topic_analytics_tab_engagement_body: Engagement measures traffic to your {labelShortName} artist page, fan page, and related web properties. Page views, referrers, and device breakdowns show how fans discover you outside streaming apps. Compare engagement bumps with social posts or newsletter sends. Requires site analytics to be enabled for your artist slug.
topic_analytics_tab_merch_body: When a Shopify or compatible merch store is linked, the Merch tab summarizes orders, revenue, and top products attributed to your artist. Use it alongside tour merch settlement in Tour Planner for a full picture of retail performance. Data sync frequency depends on the store integration. No connection yields an empty state with setup guidance.
topic_analytics_workflow_body: Analytics data typically flows from distributors and label systems into {labelShortName} on a scheduled sync. After a major release, allow up to 48 hours for full territory and earnings breakdowns. Label teams may upload supplemental files for campaigns or corrections—those imports can refresh specific tabs without waiting for the next global sync. Export CSV before meetings if you need a frozen snapshot.
topic_analytics_usecase_body: Before a quarterly label call, filter to the last 90 days and export Streaming and Earnings for talking points. Tour managers compare Territories and Events tabs to pick encore markets. Press teams watch EPK & press after sending a new kit. Accountants cross-check Earnings trends against downloaded Statements rather than treating analytics as legal payout proof.
topic_analytics_troubleshoot_body: If all tabs show no data, confirm the artist.analytics feature flag is enabled and your catalog has reported activity in the chosen date range. Partial empty tabs often mean that data source is not integrated—contact your label rather than assuming a bug. Sudden drops may reflect platform restatements or filter changes; widen the date range to verify. Export failures usually stem from ad blockers or pop-up blockers—allow downloads for the portal domain.
topic_multi_artist_overview_body: Some users manage multiple acts under one login, such as solo projects, bands, and side acts. The active artist selector in the portal header determines which profile, analytics, releases, and finance data you see. Each artist has separate settings, documents, and billing profiles. Switching artists reloads context-sensitive pages to prevent cross-posting or mislabeled submissions.
topic_multi_artist_steps_body: Click the artist name or avatar in the portal header to open the roster list. Select the act you want to work on; the URL may gain or update an artistId query parameter for deep links. Wait for the page to finish loading before editing forms—saving while a switch is in progress can target the wrong artist. Bookmark URLs with artistId when sharing links with band members who use the same account.
topic_multi_artist_troubleshoot_body: If you only see one artist but expect more, an invite may be pending or the label has not linked additional acts yet. Wrong data after switching usually means a stale tab—refresh or navigate away and back. Missing artists in the dropdown require label admin action; you cannot self-add acts. Deep links without artistId open your default or last-used artist.
topic_profile_overview_body: Your artist profile is the canonical source for biography, imagery, contacts, links, and technical riders on {labelShortName} and in exported press materials. Changes here flow into your public artist page, fan page blocks, and EPK data bindings. Keep booking and press contacts current so inquiries route correctly. Profile content is per artist; each act in your roster maintains its own profile.
topic_profile_tab_bio_body: The Bio tab holds your profile photo, short and long biographies, and optional image gallery. Write bios in the languages your audience expects; long bio supports rich text for press-ready paragraphs. Gallery images appear on your public page and can be pulled into EPK layouts. Use high-resolution, rights-cleared photos to avoid takedown or blur on large screens.
topic_profile_tab_info_body: The Info tab stores structured contact and business details such as booking email, press contact, management, and label display preferences. Separate fields prevent fan mail from mixing with fee negotiations. Some fields may be required before certain portal actions unlock. Verify emails are monitored by the right person on your team.
topic_profile_tab_links_body: Links collects outbound URLs for Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube, Bandcamp, and social networks. Valid URLs render as icons on your public artist page and fan page link rows. Order or pin primary platforms so fans see the most important destinations first. Broken links degrade completion score and fan experience—test after every domain change.
topic_profile_tab_riders_body: The Riders tab uploads stage plots, input lists, hospitality riders, and other PDFs venues request. Promoters often download these directly from your public page or EPK. Keep file names clear and versions dated when you change backline requirements. Large files may take longer to upload; stay on the page until save confirms.
topic_profile_workflow_body: Update profile fields before regenerating or sharing an EPK so press receives consistent facts and imagery. EPK builder can bind text and images to profile data—editing the profile updates bound elements on next sync or refresh. For one-off press variants, override in the EPK canvas without changing the master profile. Document major bio changes in Marketing promo notes for timeline context.
topic_profile_troubleshoot_body: If saves fail, check required fields and image size limits on the Bio tab. Photos not appearing publicly may still be in moderation or cache—allow a few minutes and hard-refresh the public page. Riders not downloading often trace to unsupported file types; use PDF. Contact your label if you lack permission to edit a tab you need for shows.
topic_epk_builder_overview_body: The EPK builder lets you design a shareable electronic press kit with drag-and-drop layout, brand typography, and live data from your profile. Export PDFs for email attachments or publish password-protected share links for journalists. Templates accelerate first-time setup while custom canvases support established acts with strict brand guidelines. Work is saved per artist.
topic_epk_builder_canvas_body: The canvas is your artboard where text, images, shapes, and data-bound blocks are placed. Layers panel controls stacking order—bring logos above backgrounds or tuck legal text behind hero photos. Snap-to-grid helps align multi-page layouts consistently. Zoom and pan for detail work on dense pages.
topic_epk_builder_toolbar_body: The toolbar provides add-element actions, undo/redo, zoom, and page management for multi-page kits. Insert headings, body text, images, dividers, and dynamic fields linked to profile or analytics snippets where supported. Use alignment tools to keep margins even across pages. Save explicitly before long editing sessions; autosave may lag on slow connections.
topic_epk_builder_assets_body: Upload brand assets, logos, and custom fonts into the assets panel for reuse across pages. Supported image formats typically include PNG and JPEG; fonts must be licensed for embedding in PDF export. Organize assets by campaign to retire outdated artwork without deleting historical EPK versions. Asset size limits apply—compress large print photos before upload.
topic_epk_builder_templates_body: Templates are starting layouts provided by {labelShortName} or saved from your own designs. Apply a template to a new EPK without overwriting an in-progress kit unless you confirm replacement. Customize colors and typography after applying to match your brand. Save your finalized layout as a private template for tour cycles.
topic_epk_builder_share_body: Generate a share URL that presents a read-only web view of your EPK for press. Optional password and expiry date restrict access to active campaigns. Rotate passwords after major leaks or when a journalist window closes. Track opens in Analytics EPK & press when enabled.
topic_epk_builder_pdf_body: Export produces a print-ready PDF matching your canvas layout for attach-and-send workflows. Embedded fonts and images depend on asset licensing—missing glyphs may fall back to system fonts in preview. Large galleries increase file size; consider a lite PDF for email and full PDF on request. Re-export after any content change so recipients never see stale stats.
topic_epk_builder_shortcuts_body: Press Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on Mac) to open the command palette for quick navigation and element actions within the builder. Standard undo/redo shortcuts apply on supported browsers. Learn shortcut hints in the help overlay to speed repetitive alignment tasks. Shortcuts do not conflict with browser find when the canvas is focused.
topic_epk_builder_workflow_body: Start from profile completeness, then pick a template, replace placeholder copy, and bind dynamic stats for current streaming highlights. Preview web share and PDF before sending to press lists. Log the share date in Marketing promo log for correlation with analytics. Archive outdated EPKs by expiring links rather than deleting historical PDFs you may need for disputes.
topic_epk_builder_troubleshoot_body: Blank preview usually means unsaved changes or expired share link—save and regenerate. PDF export stalls on very large images; reduce resolution. Fonts rendering incorrectly indicate missing upload or licensing—re-upload TTF/OTF files. If the builder does not load, disable aggressive browser extensions blocking WebGL or canvas.
topic_fan_page_overview_body: The fan page is a customizable landing page at your artist slug for campaigns, link-in-bio replacement, and newsletter capture. Blocks stack vertically—hero, music links, merch, tour dates, and more depending on enabled modules. Unpublished changes stay in draft until you submit for review if your label requires approval. Published pages are mobile-first and indexed per label SEO policy.
topic_fan_page_blocks_body: Each block type serves a purpose: hero for headline and key visual, streaming row for DSP links, newsletter for email signup, merch for store highlights, and embeds for video or playlists. Reorder blocks to match campaign priority—tour season may move dates above new single links. Disable unused blocks to shorten scroll depth. Some blocks pull live data from profile, events, or Shopify automatically.
topic_fan_page_theme_body: Theme controls background, accent, and text colors plus font pairings where customization is allowed. Contrast warnings flag combinations that fail accessibility guidelines for readability. Preview light and dark environments fans use on phones outdoors. Stay on-brand but prioritize legible body text over decorative low-contrast choices.
topic_fan_page_publish_body: Edit in draft mode, preview on desktop and mobile, then publish or submit for label review depending on trust settings. Trusted artists may go live immediately; others enter a queue until approved. Post-publish edits may require re-approval—plan campaign timing accordingly. Cache busting can take minutes before all fans see updates.
topic_fan_page_usecase_body: Point Instagram bio links to the fan page instead of a single DSP to capture emails and show merch plus dates. Run pre-save campaigns by featuring one release block with UTM-tagged buttons. Touring acts put city lists and ticket links in hero subcopy. Labels use approved fan pages as the canonical campaign URL in ads.
topic_fan_page_troubleshoot_body: 404 on public URL means unpublished or slug mismatch—confirm publish status in portal. Newsletter signups not arriving may indicate integration not configured—check with label. Merch block empty when store disconnected—relink Shopify. Review rejection emails explain required fixes before resubmitting.
topic_releases_overview_body: Releases lists your distributed and in-progress titles with status, dates, and links to detail or edit flows. See which projects are live, scheduled, or awaiting label processing. The catalog ties into analytics and calendar pre-save events when configured. Use it as the authoritative list before pitching playlists or planning release-week social posts.
topic_releases_checklist_body: The checklist highlights missing metadata, artwork issues, or approval steps blocking delivery. Items may include ISRC assignment, explicit flags, contributor credits, and marketing assets. Resolve red items before the delivery deadline to avoid street-date slips. Green checklist does not guarantee platform live times—DSPs have their own ingestion queues.
topic_releases_troubleshoot_body: Missing releases usually mean they are linked to another artist profile or still in label intake—confirm active artist. Stuck in processing beyond SLA warrants a message to your label via Messages. Cannot edit locked releases after delivery—request revisions through label ops. Analytics not attributing to a new release may need 24–72 hours post-ingestion.
topic_release_submission_overview_body: Submit a release when you are ready to deliver a single, EP, or album through {labelShortName} distribution workflows. The form captures metadata, track listing, rights information, and artwork for label review and downstream DSP delivery. Save drafts if you cannot finish in one session. Final submit notifies your label and may lock certain fields pending approval.
topic_release_submission_fields_body: Enter title, version, primary artists, featured artists, genre, release date, label imprint, and catalog number where applicable. Each track needs title, duration, explicit flag, and ISRC—new ISRCs may be auto-generated if permitted. Contributor roles (producer, writer, mixer) affect royalty splits—accuracy is legally important. Double-check spelling; DSPs display exactly what you submit.
topic_release_submission_cover_body: Upload square cover art meeting minimum 3000×3000 pixels in JPEG or PNG without transparency for most stores. Avoid explicit imagery in thumbnails if you want broader playlist eligibility—follow platform content policies. Text on cover should be legible at thumbnail size. Wrong dimensions or embedded color profiles are common rejection reasons.
topic_release_submission_workflow_body: After submit, label ops validates metadata and artwork, may request fixes via Messages, then queues delivery to DSPs. You receive portal status updates as the release moves from draft to approved to delivered. Set calendar pre-save only after approval if your label policy requires it. Keep marketing assets ready in parallel to shorten campaign lead time.
topic_release_submission_troubleshoot_bo: Upload failures often trace to artwork dimensions or file size—re-export at spec. ISRC conflicts mean the code is already used on another track—verify with your label. Cannot submit with incomplete track rows—expand each track section. Session timeout loses unsaved work—save draft frequently on long albums.
topic_video_submissions_overview_body: Video submissions register official music videos and visualizers linked to your audio releases for YouTube and partner video platforms where supported. The portal tracks review status separately from audio delivery. Approved videos may sync to channel management workflows your label operates. Keep video metadata aligned with the audio ISRC for Content ID matching.
topic_video_submissions_submit_body: Choose the linked audio release or track, provide the YouTube URL or upload per label policy, and enter title, description, and premiere date if scheduled. Declare featured artists and territory restrictions matching your licensing. Submit captions or lyric files when available for accessibility. Wait for approval before announcing premiere times publicly.
topic_video_submissions_troubleshoot_bod: Rejected YouTube links may be private, region-blocked, or not owned by your channel—verify ownership in YouTube Studio. Mismatched ISRC prevents linking—update audio metadata first. Premiere conflicts occur if audio is not live yet—coordinate street dates. Upload timeouts suggest file too large—follow codec guidance from your label.
topic_calendar_overview_body: The calendar visualizes release dates, pre-saves, milestones, and label-added events on a month or agenda view. It helps teams coordinate social teasers, ads, and show announcements around the same windows. Events may originate from submitted releases, manual label entries, or integrated tour dates. Subscribe or export where ICS feeds are offered on other pages.
topic_calendar_filters_body: Filter by event type to hide tour dates or show only pre-save campaigns during release crunch. Pre-save entries display suggested go-live dates and links once approved. Color coding distinguishes label events from artist-created items when both exist. Clear filters if the calendar appears empty despite upcoming releases.
topic_calendar_troubleshoot_body: Missing pre-save usually means release not approved or feature disabled—check Releases checklist. Wrong timezone on events shifts day boundaries—confirm profile or browser timezone. Duplicate entries can appear when tour and release systems both add the same show—hide one type via filters. ICS export empty means no events in exported range.
topic_events_overview_body: Events manages individual shows, festivals, and appearances fans should know about. Listed events appear on your public artist page and may feed the fan page tour block. Each entry includes date, venue, ticket link, and optional notes for press. This module is for fan-facing confirmed dates, not internal routing plans—that is Tour Planner.
topic_events_form_body: Enter event title, start time, door time if needed, venue name, address, city, country, and ticket URL. Optional fields cover sold-out state, age restrictions, and lineup billing position. Geocoded address improves map pin accuracy on the public site. Save as draft if details are unconfirmed—publish only when contracts and on-sale dates are firm.
topic_events_map_body: Addresses geocode via OpenStreetMap Nominatim to place pins on event maps. Verify the pin sits on the correct building—ambiguous venue names may need street address in the form. Map previews help fans plan travel. Failed geocode leaves text-only listing without map until corrected.
topic_events_export_body: Export events to ICS for import into Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, or Outlook for personal and crew scheduling. ICS is read-only fan data—not two-way sync with Tour Planner. Share exported files with band members who do not use the portal. Refresh exports after bulk edits to avoid stale show times.
topic_events_usecase_body: Use Events for public, ticketed shows fans must discover on your website. Use Tour Planner for routing, day sheets, crew, and settlements behind the scenes. A tour stop may generate a public Event when you are ready to announce. Announcing too early in Events before on-sale can frustrate fans—coordinate with marketing.
topic_events_troubleshoot_body: Event not on public page may be draft or past hidden cutoff—check publish toggle and date. Wrong venue on map—re-enter full address and save to re-geocode. Ticket link broken breaks fan conversion—test URL in incognito. Duplicate shows often come from manual entry plus import—delete or merge duplicates.
topic_tour_planner_overview_body: Tour Planner is an operational hub for tours and multi-date runs: routes, stops, crew, tasks, contacts, and merch settlements. Data can work offline on supported routes with later sync. It complements public Events by holding private notes, financial splits, and travel logistics. One tour contains many stops; stops link to day sheets and optional public event publishing.
topic_tour_planner_stops_body: Each stop represents a show or travel day with venue, load-in, soundcheck, set times, and hospitality notes in the day sheet. Attach settlement worksheets per stop for door deals and merch splits. Mark stops confirmed, hold, or cancelled for crew visibility. Reorder stops when routing changes—downstream mileage recalculates when geocoding is enabled.
topic_tour_planner_route_body: Route view maps stop sequence and estimates drive distances and fuel costs using geocoded locations. Use it to detect backtracking or unrealistic drive days. Fuel estimates are planning aids—not tax receipts. Update addresses on stops before trusting mileage totals for budget sign-off.
topic_tour_planner_tasks_body: Tasks assign actionable items to crew roles with due dates—visa applications, backline rentals, press days, and more. Filter open tasks per stop or tour-wide. Completed tasks archive for the next leg’s template reuse. Notifications depend on portal message settings.
topic_tour_planner_contacts_body: Store promoter, venue, agent, and local fixer contacts per stop or tour. Quick-copy phone and email on site reduces day-of chaos. Contacts do not sync to public Events—keep sensitive numbers here only. Import contact sheets from CSV when onboarding a new tour manager mid-run.
topic_tour_planner_crew_body: Crew lists band and road personnel with roles, per diem notes, and rooming preferences where tracked. Share relevant day sheet sections without exposing full financial tabs to every member. Crew data may feed task assignments. Update roster when subs join mid-tour for accurate call times.
topic_tour_planner_merch_body: Record nightly merch sales, venue cuts, and payment method per stop. Settlement totals can align with Analytics merch and settlement ledger for reconciliation. Enter adjustments for comps and damaged goods to keep splits honest. Export settlement summaries for accounting after the run.
topic_tour_planner_import_body: Import stops from CSV templates or bridge confirmed concerts from Events to avoid double typing. Map columns carefully on first import—save mapping presets for recurring promoters. Bridge imports may create draft stops requiring day sheet completion. Validate dates after import—timezone offsets cause common off-by-one errors.
topic_tour_planner_offline_body: Tour Planner supports offline editing on enabled devices using local storage, syncing when connectivity returns. Edit day sheets and tasks in airplane mode during flights. Conflicts arise if two users edit the same stop offline—server returns 409 and prompts merge. Sync before leaving venue Wi‑Fi if crew shares one tour login.
topic_tour_planner_troubleshoot_body: 409 conflict means concurrent edits—refresh, compare versions, and reapply changes. Missing stops after sync usually failed mid-upload—retry from online state. Map not loading offline is expected—cached tiles are limited. Feature disabled means artist.tour_planner flag off—contact label. Dexie storage errors on private browsing—use normal browser profile.
topic_marketing_overview_body: Marketing centralizes promo campaign notes, playlist pitching logs, and downloadable press assets for your active artist. It gives labels and artists a shared timeline of what was pitched, when, and outcomes if recorded. Tie activities to releases in Analytics Events for impact review. Not a replacement for ad platform dashboards—supplemental narrative context.
topic_marketing_promo_body: Log playlist pitches, blog outreach, radio adds, and social campaigns with dates, targets, and free-text results. Consistent logging prevents duplicate pitches to the same curator. Filter by release to report during label meetings. Attach URLs to proof of placement when available.
topic_marketing_assets_body: Upload press photos, one-sheets, and audio snippets for label and media use. Organize by campaign folder and version. Assets here may differ from profile gallery—optimized for download packs. Respect file size limits; provide ZIP for bulk photo drops.
topic_marketing_troubleshoot_body: Cannot upload may mean artist.marketing flag disabled. Missing historical promos were never migrated—backfill manually if needed. Duplicate log entries clutter reports—edit or archive old rows. Download links expiring relate to presigned URL lifetime—re-download promptly or request reissue.
topic_billing_overview_body: The billing profile stores legal name, address, IBAN or bank details, tax IDs, and VAT status for royalty payouts and invoicing. Incomplete billing blocks invoice creation and may delay SOS payments per label policy. Kleinunternehmer and reverse-charge settings affect PDF invoice footers. One billing profile per artist entity—update when ownership changes.
topic_billing_fields_body: Legal entity name must match bank account holder for successful transfers. IBAN is validated for format; micro-deposits may be used by finance for first payout. VAT ID required for EU B2B invoices above thresholds. Country drives tax template on generated PDFs—choose incorporation country, not touring country.
topic_billing_workflow_body: After initial save, label finance may verify details before enabling invoice gates. Changes mid-period can pause payouts until re-verified—plan updates between SOS cycles. Billing completeness checks run when you open Invoices or Statements. Keep PDF proofs of tax registration handy for audit requests.
topic_billing_troubleshoot_body: Incomplete banner persists when required fields empty or IBAN fails checksum—re-enter carefully without spaces if validation errors. Payouts still failing after correct IBAN may be bank-side—contact label finance with SWIFT/BIC if cross-border. VAT validation errors on VIES mean ID typo or inactive registration. Cannot edit locked fields when invoice in flight—wait until period closes.
topic_statements_overview_body: Statements of Sales (SOS) are official period summaries of royalties earned, adjustments, and balances due from your label. Each statement moves through workflow statuses from draft to approved to paid or invoiced. Download PDF or detail files for accounting. SOS figures supersede analytics earnings for legal and tax reporting.
topic_statements_statuses_body: Draft statements are preliminary and may change before label approval. Approved means figures are locked for artist review and invoicing. Invoiced indicates you submitted an invoice against the statement. Paid confirms settlement—match to bank deposits by reference and period dates.
topic_statements_download_body: Click download on an approved statement to receive a presigned URL valid for a short time. Save PDFs locally immediately—links expire for security. Bulk periods may offer CSV detail for line-level tracks. If download fails, disable pop-up blockers and retry before URL expiry.
topic_statements_invoice_body: From an approved statement, start invoice creation to bill the label for your share per contract. Line items may pre-fill from statement totals. Billing profile must be complete or the flow stops at the gate. After submit, track payment via Invoices list and statement status.
topic_statements_troubleshoot_body: No statements yet may mean periods still processing or artist.statements flag off. Draft never advancing needs label finance action—send a Message. Download 403 means expired link—refresh page and download again. Totals disagreeing with analytics—expected due to adjustments; trust SOS for payouts.
topic_invoices_overview_body: Invoices lets you create, send, and track bills to your label for royalty periods, merch settlements, or approved free-form services. SOS-linked invoices pull authoritative amounts; free generator supports reimbursements with manual lines. Email delivery notifies label AP contacts. Status tracks from draft through paid or cancelled.
topic_invoices_list_body: The list shows number, date, amount, status (draft, sent, paid, cancelled), and linked SOS period if any. Filter paid for tax year summaries. Cancelled invoices remain for audit with watermark. Click a row for PDF preview and resend options.
topic_invoices_create_body: Choose SOS-linked or free generator, confirm billing profile, add line items with descriptions and net amounts, then preview PDF. Attach optional notes for purchase order references label requires. Send emails to configured AP addresses; copy yourself for records. Save draft if awaiting final SOS approval.
topic_invoices_sos_body: SOS-linked flow reads statement query parameters to pre-select period and amount due per your deal. Editing totals away from SOS may require label override—avoid unless contract allows partial billing. Multiple SOS periods cannot merge on one invoice unless finance permits. Statement must be approved status to start.
topic_invoices_free_body: Free generator builds invoices without SOS for tour reimbursements, sync fees, or other agreed line items. You enter recipient, lines, tax treatment, and payment terms manually. Misuse for royalty periods bypassing SOS is discouraged—finance may reject. Keep supporting receipts in Documents vault.
topic_invoices_troubleshoot_body: Send blocked by billing gate—complete Billing profile. PDF missing logo or wrong tax text—update VAT settings and regenerate. Email bounced—verify label AP email with your contact. Paid status not updating is label-side bookkeeping lag—not automatic bank sync.
topic_messages_overview_body: Messages is the threaded inbox between you and your {labelShortName} label team. Use it for release corrections, marketing coordination, finance questions, and interview requests that need a paper trail. Unread badges appear in portal navigation when new replies arrive. Threads are per artist context when managing multiple acts.
topic_messages_reply_body: Open a thread to read history oldest-to-newest, attach files if allowed, and send replies that notify label users. Keep one topic per thread when possible for faster routing. Quote release names and dates in first message to reduce back-and-forth. Mark read implicitly by opening; no separate archive in basic workflow.
topic_messages_troubleshoot_body: Unread count stuck—hard refresh portal. Cannot attach—file exceeds limit or type blocked—use Documents link instead. No reply in days—label SLA varies; escalate via secondary contact if urgent release block. Missing threads after artist switch—confirm correct artist selected in header.
topic_interviews_overview_body: Interview requests arrive from media outlets via {labelShortName} press workflows for your acceptance or decline. Review outlet, proposed questions, deadline, and format (written, phone, video). Accepted interviews may sync calendar holds and press asset access. Declined requests notify outlet with optional reason.
topic_interviews_status_body: Pending awaits your response—respond before deadline to protect outlet relationships. Accepted locks basic details and may generate tasks for press prep. Rejected closes the request; outlets may not re-submit immediately. Completed may archive after publish date for analytics correlation.
topic_interviews_troubleshoot_body: No requests visible means none routed to your artist or press module disabled. Cannot accept—profile press contact missing—update Info tab. Double booking—check Calendar before accepting overlapping slots. Outlet cancelled externally—label may mark cancelled; no action needed from you.
topic_documents_overview_body: Documents is a private vault for contracts, split sheets, GEMA registrations, tax letters, and tour paperwork tied to your artist. Files are not public—label and authorized users access per permissions. Use categories for fast retrieval during audits or disputes. Retention follows label policy; do not delete executed contracts without counsel.
topic_documents_categories_body: Categories include contracts, split agreements, GEMA, tax, riders, insurance, and general. Pick the closest category on upload for filter sidebar grouping. Mis-categorized files are hard to find during SOS disputes—fix metadata after upload. Some categories trigger label notifications on new uploads.
topic_documents_upload_body: Drag PDF, DOCX, or common image types up to 20 MB per file unless label raised limit. Add descriptive titles and effective dates in notes. Virus scan may delay availability by seconds. Batch upload tour contracts after each leg for centralized backup.
topic_documents_troubleshoot_body: Upload fails at 20 MB—compress PDF or split exhibits. artist.documents flag off removes module entirely. Preview not loading—browser PDF plugin blocked—download instead. Missing file after upload—refresh; if still gone, retry on stable connection.
topic_settings_overview_body: Settings controls your portal user account: password, interface language, and notification preferences where available. These apply across all artists linked to your login. They do not change artist profile public data—that is under Profile. Review security settings after crew turnover on shared machines.
topic_settings_password_body: Enter current password, new password meeting complexity rules, and confirmation. Log out other sessions if option offered after change. Use a unique password with manager apps. Forgot password flows run on login page, not here.
topic_settings_language_body: Switch between English (en) and German (de) for portal UI labels and help content. Language is stored on your user profile and persists across devices. Help articles and glossary respect the same locale. Public fan pages use separate language settings on content blocks.
topic_settings_troubleshoot_body: Password change fails on mismatch or reused weak password—follow error hints. Language revert on login means save did not complete—click save and wait for toast. Notifications missing may be email deliverability—check spam and whitelist label domain.
topic_onboarding_overview_body: Onboarding walks new artists through linking an account, confirming legal name, and completing minimum profile data before full portal access. Steps adapt if you arrived via invite versus self-registration. Finishing onboarding unlocks dashboard and profile editing. Skipping steps may trigger completion reminders on the dashboard.
topic_onboarding_steps_body: Typical steps: accept terms, upload photo, write short bio, add streaming links, and confirm billing basics if payouts expected. Each step validates before continue—fix inline errors rather than skipping. Photo and bio improve public page within minutes after save. Return to onboarding checklist from dashboard completion widget if interrupted.
topic_onboarding_troubleshoot_body: Stuck on linking means no invite token—request invite from label. Photo upload loops on unsupported HEIC—convert to JPEG. Cannot finish billing—optional until first SOS—skip if allowed. Redirect loop—clear cookies and retry login.
topic_accept_invite_overview_body: Accept invite connects your user account to an artist roster entry when the label sends an email invitation. The flow verifies token, shows which act you are joining, and may require login or signup. Successful accept adds the artist to your header switcher. Declining or ignoring leaves invite expired for security.
topic_accept_invite_steps_body: Open the invite link from email before expiry, sign in or create account with matching email if required, review artist name and role, then confirm accept. You land on dashboard or onboarding depending on profile completeness. Forward invites only to trusted team members—accept binds their login. One invite per email token—request resend if expired.
topic_accept_invite_troubleshoot_body: Expired token needs label to resend invite—links are time-boxed. Email mismatch blocks accept—use invited address or ask label to update. Already accepted shows duplicate message—switch artist in header. Invite opens blank on mobile mail clients—copy URL to desktop browser.
topic_access_gate_overview_body: Access gate screens appear when your account exists but label approval is pending or an artist link is not yet active. You may see limited navigation or a holding message instead of dashboard content. This protects unreleased roster info and prevents premature submissions. Approval is label-side—portal cannot self-grant access.
topic_access_gate_troubleshoot_body: Waiting more than expected SLA—contact label via alternate email if Messages unavailable. Wrong gate after invite accept—logout and login to refresh claims. Approved but still gated—hard refresh; rare cache of JWT requires re-auth. Multiple accounts—ensure you logged into invited email not personal duplicate.
topic_feature_flags_overview_body: Feature flags control which portal modules are visible per artist contract and rollout stage. Disabled modules hide navigation items and return unavailable states if accessed via bookmark. Flags like artist.analytics or artist.tour_planner are set by label administrators—not toggled by artists. Understanding flags explains empty menus versus bugs.
topic_feature_flags_modules_body: artist.analytics unlocks Analytics. artist.epk_builder unlocks EPK builder. artist.fan_page unlocks fan page editor. artist.calendar unlocks release calendar. artist.tour_planner unlocks Tour Planner. artist.statements and artist.invoices gate finance areas. artist.documents enables vault. artist.marketing opens promo tools. Missing flag means module not contracted or not yet rolled out.
topic_feature_flags_troubleshoot_body: Module unavailable despite expecting access—confirm with label which flags apply to your deal. Deep link to disabled module shows friendly unavailable page—not an error to report. Flag enabled mid-day may need logout/login to refresh menu. Beta flags may flicker during maintenance—retry later.
topic_offline_overview_body: Parts of the portal work offline when a service worker caches shell assets and enabled routes sync local data. Help and Tour Planner are primary offline-capable areas; most finance and submission flows require connectivity. Offline indicator in UI shows when edits queue for sync. Do not clear browser data mid-tour or unsynced day sheets may be lost.
topic_offline_routes_body: Help remains readable offline after first visit for reference in venues without Wi‑Fi. Tour Planner allows stop and task edits with Dexie-backed storage until reconnect. Dashboard, analytics, and invoices are online-only by design. Navigation to blocked routes shows explanatory banner instead of broken pages.
topic_offline_troubleshoot_body: Nav blocked offline is intentional for online-only modules—do not force URL entry. Sync not running—check connection and service worker registration in browser settings. Stale help content—open help online once to refresh cache. Private mode disables persistent offline storage—use standard profile on tour buses with shared tablets.
glossary_active_artist_definition: The artist profile currently selected in the portal header. All data views, edits, and submissions apply to this act until you switch to another artist in your roster.
glossary_billing_profile_definition: Legal and bank information stored for payouts and invoicing, including name, address, IBAN, and VAT or tax identifiers for the artist entity.
glossary_co_tour_definition: A tour leg shared with another headliner or package where routing, settlements, or merch splits may differ from a solo headlining run.
glossary_day_sheet_definition: A one-page schedule for a tour stop listing load-in, soundcheck, set times, contacts, and notes for crew and artists.
glossary_epk_definition: Electronic press kit—a shareable package of bio, photos, music highlights, and stats for journalists and promoters, often built in the EPK builder.
glossary_fan_page_definition: A customizable public landing page at your artist slug (@slug) for links, newsletter signup, merch, and tour promotion.
glossary_feature_flag_definition: A per-artist switch that enables or hides portal modules such as analytics, tour planner, or statements based on contract and rollout.
glossary_isrc_definition: International Standard Recording Code—a unique identifier assigned to each audio track for royalty tracking across platforms.
glossary_merch_settlement_definition: Night-of or end-of-show accounting of merchandise sales, venue share, and payouts recorded in Tour Planner or finance tools.
glossary_presigned_url_definition: A time-limited secure download link for statement PDFs and other private files, expiring after a short period for security.
glossary_promo_log_definition: A chronological record in Marketing of playlist pitches, press outreach, and campaigns tied to releases.
glossary_settlement_ledger_definition: A running financial record of tour settlements, carry-forward balances, and adjustments connected to analytics and SOS.
glossary_sos_definition: Official royalty statement for a period summarizing earnings, deductions, and amount due—used as the basis for artist invoicing.
glossary_stop_definition: A single show or tour date entry in Tour Planner with venue, day sheet, and optional link to a public Event listing.
glossary_track_definition: In Tour Planner context, a planned route or leg of travel between stops; in music context, an individual audio recording with its own ISRC.
